content.text:
{
  "dimension": "coaching-stance",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach demonstrates VIEW throughout: pace is slow, questions are single and unhurried, and there's no agenda to fix or give the script the client repeatedly requests. The coach declines the bait at turn 9 (where client asks for words) and instead gently notices the move away (turn 10) — that requires impartiality and trust in the client. Wonder is present in the phenomenon-tracking ('I notice you moved pretty quickly toward the words') rather than generic probing. Empathy in turn 8 names both fears without performing sympathy. The coach holds silence-space (short questions, room for client pauses) rather than filling. There's no preacher-mode, no validation noises, no improvement language. The 'There you go again' in turn 12 is undefended and warm without being saccharine. Vulnerability/first-person disclosure from coach is the one slightly weaker leg, but the overall stance is clearly genuine non-attached curiosity, not technique-on-rails.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Spare, open question. No rush to content."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Goes under the guilt with genuine curiosity, not investigative pressure."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Reflects empathy accurately, then offers a question rather than reassurance. Could read as slightly leading but lands as openness."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Declines the script-bait — strong evidence of no agenda to help/fix. Tracks the actual phenomenon (the move away)."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Stays with what just happened rather than analyzing. Warm, undefended, no performance."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Asks for the want, slowly, before the caveat. Pace matches what the client's system needs."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["linguistic-caveat", "state-the-want-not-the-action", "empathize-with-fear-under-constricted-boundary", "view"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["does-saying-it-increase-your-love", "empowered-vs-power"],
  "notes": "Stance is clearly VIEW. Missing love-test is a methodology/intervention matter, not a stance failure. Coach's own first-person vulnerability ('I notice I want to...') never appears explicitly but is not needed here given how undefended the questions are."
}
